content.problemStatement:
Alice (uniformly and independently) randomly picks two integers a,ba, ba,b from the range [1,104][1,10^4][1,104], and writes down the values of a+ba + ba+b, a−ba - ba−b, a⋅ba \cdot ba⋅b and ⌊ab⌋\lfloor \frac{a}{b} \rfloor⌊ba​⌋ (integer division) in some random order. Unfortunately, she forgot the values of aaa and bbb. You need to help her to find out if there exists two integers a,ba, ba,b such that 1≤a,b≤1041 \leq a, b \leq 10^41≤a,b≤104 and a+ba + ba+b, a−ba - ba−b, a⋅ba \cdot ba⋅b, ⌊ab⌋\lfloor \frac{a}{b} \rfloor⌊ba​⌋ are the numbers she has written down.

If a solution exists, it is guaranteed to be unique.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of testcases. The description of TTT testcases follows.
Each testcase consists of a single line of input, containing four space-separated integers A,B,C,DA, B, C, DA,B,C,D — the values written down by Alice. It is guaranteed that at most one of the four numbers A,B,C,DA,B,C,DA,B,C,D will be negative.


Output Format

For each testcase, output in a single line, separated by a space, the two numbers Alice has chosen **in order** (i.e, if the solution is a=1a = 1a=1 and b=2b = 2b=2, print 1 21 \ 21 2 and not 2 12 \ 12 1). If there does not exist any such choice of integers, print −1-1−1 **twice**, separated by a space, instead.
constraints: At most one of A, B, C, D is negative
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10^5), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated integers A, B, C, D – the four numbers written down by Alice (in arbitrary order). It is guaranteed that at most one of the four numbers is negative.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with two integers a and b (1 ≤ a, b ≤ 10^4) in the order a b if a unique pair exists. If no such pair exists output "-1 -1".

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: the numbers correspond to a-b=-1, a*b=72, floor(a/b)=0, a+b=17, giving a=8, b=9. Second test case: no pair (a,b) in [1,10000] produces the four numbers.

solution.approach: For each test case we try all 4! = 24 ways to assign the four given numbers to the four expressions sum = a+b, diff = a-b, prod = a*b and div = floor(a/b). For a particular assignment we can recover a and b from sum and diff: a = (sum + diff) / 2, b = (sum - diff) / 2. Both must be integers, lie in [1, 10^4] and satisfy a*b == prod and a / b (integer division) == div. As soon as a valid pair is found we output it (the statement guarantees uniqueness). If none of the permutations work we output -1 -1. The work per test case is constant (24 permutations, O(1) each), so the total complexity is O(T).
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – 24 constant‑time checks per test case.
